WebContent.title: WebContent
WebContent.description: Manages content for the public-facing landing page of AssetTrack AI.

id.description: Unique identifier for the WebContent entity. Expected to be 'config_principal'.

hero.description: Hero section content including title, subtitle, and background image.

services.description: List of services offered, each with an icon, title, and description.

publicStats.description: Public statistics displayed on the landing page.

User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the AssetTrack AI platform, including their role and signature.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity (Firebase Auth UID).

role.description: Role of the user within the system.

signatureStorageUrl.description: URL to the user's stored digital signature image in cloud storage.

isFirstLogin.description: Indicates if it's the user's first login, used for initial setup or signature capture.

Asset.title: Asset
Asset.description: Details of an industrial asset managed by Energy Engine España.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Asset entity (id_bien).

airportId.description: Identifier for the airport where the asset is located.

sectorId.description: Identifier for the specific sector within the airport.

category.description: Category of the asset (e.g., Energy, BHS, Climate).

brandModel.description: Brand and model of the asset.

power.description: Power specification of the asset (e.g., '300 kW').

status.description: Current operational status of the asset.

technicalSpecifications.description: Detailed technical specifications or 'ficha técnica' of the asset, including components like filters, batteries, etc.

ReportLog.title: ReportLog
ReportLog.description: Logs detailed information about an inspection report, including times, inspection findings, and location.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ReportLog entity (id_informe).

assetId.description: Reference to the Asset being inspected. (Relationship: Asset 1:N ReportLog)

technicianId.description: Reference to the User (technician) who performed the inspection. (Relationship: User 1:N ReportLog)

times.description: Timestamps related to the inspection duration.

inspectionDetails.description: Detailed findings of the inspection, mapping a component/field to its status.

location.description: Geographical location where the inspection was performed.

needsList.description: A list of items (e.g., parts, services) identified as 'OFE' (Ofertar) during the inspection, requiring action by a supervisor.

GlobalStats.title: GlobalStats
GlobalStats.description: Aggregated statistics for the entire system, updated periodically or via Cloud Functions.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GlobalStats entity. Expected to be 'global_config'.

totalMonthlyHours.description: Total accumulated man-hours worked across all inspections for the current month.

downAssets.description: Current count of assets marked as 'PAR' (Parada) or otherwise non-operational.

completedTasksToday.description: Number of inspection tasks completed today.

Multimedia.title: Multimedia
Multimedia.description: References to multimedia files (e.g., photos) uploaded as part of an inspection report.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Multimedia entity.

reportId.description: Reference to the ReportLog this multimedia is associated with. (Relationship: ReportLog 1:N Multimedia)

storageUrl.description: URL to the multimedia file stored in cloud storage.

description.description: Optional description or context for the multimedia file.

isEvidenceForOFE.description: Indicates if this multimedia file serves as evidence for an 'OFE' (Ofertar) marked item in the inspection report.

definition.description: Public content for the landing page of AssetTrack AI. The document ID is expected to be 'config_principal'. Includes denormalized public statistics from `GlobalStats` for independent public read access, aligning with the `stats_publicas` requirement. The schema properties `hero`, `services`, and `publicStats` should be treated as structured objects/arrays despite potentially being typed as `string` in the provided entity schema.

User profiles, indexed by their Firebase Authentication UID. Contains personal details, signature URL, and first login status. User roles ('admin', 'gerente', 'inspector') are managed in separate `/roles` collections for Database-Backed Access Control (DBAC) as per design principles, not directly in this document for authorization checks.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the user.

definition.description: Dedicated collection for identifying users with the 'admin' role. The document ID corresponds to the user's UID. This facilitates Authorization Independence (DBAC) by allowing simple `exists()` checks in security rules to determine admin privileges without relying on custom claims or document `get()` operations.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the user with the 'admin' role.

definition.description: Dedicated collection for identifying users with the 'gerente' role. The document ID corresponds to the user's UID. This facilitates Authorization Independence (DBAC) by allowing simple `exists()` checks in security rules to determine gerente privileges without relying on custom claims or document `get()` operations.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the user with the 'gerente' role.

definition.description: Dedicated collection for identifying users with the 'inspector' role. The document ID corresponds to the user's UID. This facilitates Authorization Independence (DBAC) by allowing simple `exists()` checks in security rules to determine inspector privileges without relying on custom claims or document `get()` operations.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the user with the 'inspector' role.

definition.description: Master list of industrial assets. All authenticated users with appropriate roles (Admin, Gerente, Inspector) can read these documents to view asset details, supporting efficient global `list` operations. Only users with the 'admin' role can create, update, or delete assets. Includes fields like `airportId` and `sectorId` for potential filtering and access control based on location.

params.name: assetId
params.description: Unique identifier for the asset (e.g., 'id_bien' like M-3209).

definition.description: Detailed inspection reports. Each document contains a `technicianId` field, which is used for ownership-based authorization, allowing inspectors to create and read their own reports. Admin and Gerente roles have global `list` and `read` access. Only Admin can `update` reports, and `delete` is strictly forbidden to maintain auditability. This structure supports QAPs by allowing global queries for management while enforcing individual `read` ownership.

params.name: reportId
params.description: Unique identifier for the inspection report (e.g., 'id_informe').

definition.description: Multimedia files (e.g., photos) associated with a specific inspection report. Each document includes denormalized `reportId` and `reportOwnerId` (the `technicianId` from the parent report) to achieve Authorization Independence, enabling direct ownership checks in rules without a `get()` operation on the parent report. This ensures atomic creation and secure access for inspectors to their own report's multimedia.

params.name: reportId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent inspection report.

params.name: multimediaId
params.description: Unique identifier for the multimedia file.

definition.description: Aggregated system-wide statistics for management dashboards. The document ID is expected to be 'global_config'. This collection is primarily managed by Cloud Functions that update counters based on new `ReportLog` entries. Read access is granted to Admin and Gerente roles. Public statistics are denormalized into `web_content` for public display.

params.name: configId
params.description: The unique identifier for the global statistics configuration document (e.g., 'global_config').

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for AssetTrack AI prioritizes Authorization Independence and DBAC (Database-Backed Access Control) to ensure secure, scalable, and debuggable security rules. The core strategy involves denormalization of authorization context and structural segregation.

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**
1.  **Roles:** Instead of relying on Firebase Custom Claims (which would tie authorization logic to `request.auth.token.role` and contradict the 'DBAC (No Custom Claims)' principle), user roles (`admin`, `gerente`, `inspector`) are explicitly denormalized into dedicated top-level collections (e.g., `/roles/admin/{userId}`). Authorization rules can then perform a simple `exists()` check on these collections (e.g., `exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/roles/admin/$(request.auth.uid))`) without complex `get()` calls, enabling atomic operations and clear intent.
2.  **Multimedia Ownership:** For `Multimedia` documents, which are subcollections of `ReportLog`, the `reportOwnerId` (the `technicianId` of the parent report) and `reportId` are denormalized into each `Multimedia` document. This allows security rules to verify ownership and association directly on the `Multimedia` document (e.g., `resource.data.reportOwnerId == request.auth.uid`) without needing to perform a `get()` operation on the parent `ReportLog` document. This is critical for atomic writes and simplified rules for subcollections.
3.  **Public Content:** The `WebContent` collection, specifically the `config_principal` document, directly stores `publicStats`. This prevents the public-facing landing page from needing to query internal `GlobalStats` for public information, ensuring independent read access.

**QAPs (Queries as Authorization Primitives) and Structural Segregation:**
1.  **Global Data (e.g., Assets):** The `/assets/{assetId}` collection is designed with a homogeneous security posture, allowing all authenticated users (Admin, Gerente, Inspector via their respective roles) to `read` all assets, while `Admin` has `write` access. This supports efficient `list` operations for dashboards and asset selection without needing complex `get()`s or client-side filtering for basic visibility.
2.  **ReportLog Access:** The `/reports/{reportId}` collection serves as the central repository for all inspection reports. `Admin` and `Gerente` roles have global `list` and `read` access to all reports, enabling their dashboard views. For `Inspectors`, the `technicianId` field within each `ReportLog` document allows security rules to enforce ownership for individual `read` and `create` operations (`resource.data.technicianId == request.auth.uid`). While a `list` operation by an inspector might technically return all report IDs (depending on the rule), the `read` rule ensures they can only fetch the full content of their own reports. Client-side filtering is expected for inspectors to display only their own reports, as enforcing `list` filters in rules is not directly supported by Firestore and violates the principle of rules not being filters. The structure allows for secure `read` of individual documents based on ownership.
3.  **Private User Data:** The `/users/{userId}` path ensures private data is path-based and accessible only by the owning user (`request.auth.uid == userId`) or an `admin`.

**Consistency and Predictability:** Semantic naming conventions are used (e.g., `assets`, `reports`, `multimedia`), and wildcards are descriptive (`{assetId}`, `{reportId}`). Explicit fields like `technicianId` and `reportOwnerId` clearly indicate ownership and simplify rule writing.
